basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Fuṣṣilat — “Expounded in Detail,” also called Ḥā Mīm al-Sajda — unfolds a Book whose verses are made distinct and clear, an Arabic Qurʾān “for a people who know.” The sūra sets up the signs of creation: the earth established in measured days, the mountain-anchors, and above all that heaven which was but smoke, summoned to come “willingly or unwillingly” and answering: “We come obedient” — figure of the whole creation submissive to its Lord. At the heart of the sūra shines the verse of uprightness: “Those who say, Our Lord is God, then stand firm (istaqāmū) — the angels descend upon them: fear not, nor grieve.” Qushayrī makes of istiqāma the highest of stations: not an isolated act but constant firmness upon all the conditions of faith, to the last breath; and he grades its faces, from the renunciant to the lover who desires nothing apart from the Beloved. The sūra teaches one to repel evil with what is better, “and behold, the one between whom and you was enmity becomes a warm friend” — a beauty of character Qushayrī ties to futuwwa, spiritual chivalry. It culminates upon the promise of unveiling: “We will show them Our signs in the horizons and in their own souls, until it becomes clear to them that it is the Truth” — and Qushayrī reads in it that the horizons are for the scholars, and the soul for the knowers, who experience there the very states of the path: contraction and expansion, veiling and attraction. The Lord, Witness and Encompassing, suffices.
themes: the verses expounded and the Arabic Qurʾān
uprightness (istiqāma) and the descent of the angels
the signs in the horizons and the souls

versets.ar: حم
versets.tr: ḥā mīm
versets.trad: Ḥā. Mīm.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī has the Real speak through these two letters as through an oath: “By My truth and My life, by My glory in My attributes and in My Essence: this is a sending-down from the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.” The isolated letters open the sūra upon the mystery of the Word, whose secret none penetrates save the One who sent it down.

mots.def: opening letters of seven sūras, the Ḥawāmīm.

versets.ar: تَنزِيلٌ مِّنَ الرَّحْمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ
versets.tr: tanzīlun mina r-raḥmāni r-raḥīmi
versets.trad: A sending-down from the All-Merciful, the Compassionate,
versets.commentaire: The Book descends from the two Names of mercy: al-Raḥmān and al-Raḥīm. Qushayrī stresses that the Word comes from pure grace, not from severity: it is out of tenderness that the Real addresses His servants. The source of the Qurʾān is therefore mercy itself: whoever receives it, receives it from the hand of the Compassionate.

mots.racine: ن ز ل
mots.def: that which is made to descend gradually.

mots.racine: ر ح م

versets.ar: كِتَابٌ فُصِّلَتْ آيَاتُهُ قُرْآنًا عَرَبِيًّا لِّقَوْمٍ يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: kitābun fuṣṣilat āyātuhū qurʾānan ʿarabiyyan li-qawmin yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: a Book whose verses are expounded in detail, an Arabic Qurʾān for a people who know,
versets.commentaire: “Its verses are detailed” (fuṣṣilat): their meanings and indications are made distinct and clear. Yet Qushayrī marks a decisive nuance: the proof is set up for all, but the insight that pierces its meaning belongs only to those who know — not to the rejecters blinded by their denial. The Book is offered to the whole; only the knowing heart receives its light.

mots.racine: ك ت ب
ف ص ل
mots.def: faṣṣala: to set forth distinctly, to articulate.

mots.racine: ق ر أ / ع ر ب
mots.def: a recitation in the clear tongue of the Arabs.

mots.racine: ع ل م
mots.def: those endowed with knowledge and discernment.

versets.ar: بَشِيرًا وَنَذِيرًا فَأَعْرَضَ أَكْثَرُهُمْ فَهُمْ لَا يَسْمَعُونَ
versets.tr: bashīran wa-nadhīran fa-aʿraḍa aktharuhum fa-hum lā yasmaʿūna
versets.trad: a bearer of glad tidings and a warner; yet most of them turn away and do not listen.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī splits the two faces of the Book according to its recipients: “a bringer of glad tidings” (bashīr) for those We have elected and chosen; “a warner” (nadhīr) for those We have set up and whom We have blinded to the witnessing of Our signs. The turning-away of the many does not escape the decree: they remain fixed where Our will has placed them. Thus the same Word becomes light for one, veil for another.

mots.racine: ب ش ر
ن ذ ر
ع ر ض
س م ع
mots.def: samiʿa: to hear, to give ear.

versets.ar: وَقَالُوا قُلُوبُنَا فِي أَكِنَّةٍ مِّمَّا تَدْعُونَا إِلَيْهِ وَفِي آذَانِنَا وَقْرٌ وَمِن بَيْنِنَا وَبَيْنِكَ حِجَابٌ فَاعْمَلْ إِنَّنَا عَامِلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qālū qulūbunā fī akinnatin mimmā tadʿūnā ilayhi wa-fī ādhāninā waqrun wa-min bayninā wa-baynika ḥijābun fa-ʿmal innanā ʿāmilūna
versets.trad: They say: “Our hearts are under coverings from that to which you call us; in our ears is deafness, and between us and you is a veil. So act — we too are acting.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī perceives the subtlety of their admission: they say this out of scorn and mockery. Yet had they said it with clear sight, it would be a profession of unity — for to acknowledge that a veil of human darkness separates them from the one Reality would be the first step of a plea for grace. But they glory in it without realizing the heart, and so fall under disgrace, for having missed the inward verification.

mots.racine: ق ل ب / ك ن ن
أ ذ ن / و ق ر
mots.sens: and in our ears a deafness

mots.racine: ح ج ب
ع م ل

versets.ar: قُلْ إِنَّمَا أَنَا بَشَرٌ مِّثْلُكُمْ يُوحَىٰ إِلَيَّ أَنَّمَا إِلَٰهُكُمْ إِلَٰهٌ وَاحِدٌ فَاسْتَقِيمُوا إِلَيْهِ وَاسْتَغْفِرُوهُ وَوَيْلٌ لِّلْمُشْرِكِينَ
versets.tr: qul innamā anā basharun mithlukum yūḥā ilayya annamā ilāhukum ilāhun wāḥidun fa-staqīmū ilayhi wa-staghfirūhu wa-waylun li-l-mushrikīna
versets.trad: Say: “I am only a mortal like you. It is revealed to me that your God is one God. So go straight to Him and seek His forgiveness.” And woe to those who associate,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī has the Prophet say: “I am a man like you in form, frame, essence and make; but what distinguishes me from you is that it is revealed to me that your God is one God: the privilege comes from Him, not from me.” And he adds the proof of long experience: “I have lived among you a lifetime, you have known me an age, and you have never caught in me a single misstep nor found in my speech the least alloy of falsehood.” The command, then, is twofold: stand upright in His obedience and surrender to His command. Blessed is he who answers; woe to him who persists.

mots.racine: ب ش ر / م ث ل
أ ل ه / و ح د
ق و م
mots.def: istaqāma: to stand upright, to persevere in rectitude.

mots.racine: غ ف ر
ش ر ك
mots.def: mushrik: one who associates partners with God.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْتُونَ الزَّكَاةَ وَهُم بِالْآخِرَةِ هُمْ كَافِرُونَ
versets.tr: alladhīna lā yuʾtūna z-zakāta wa-hum bi-l-ākhirati hum kāfirūna
versets.trad: those who do not give the alms and who disbelieve in the Hereafter.
versets.commentaire: The associators are described by two traits: refusing the alms and denying the life to come. Qushayrī sees in avarice the sign of a heart clinging to the world, and in the denial of the Hereafter the root of that avarice: whoever does not believe in the return strips himself of nothing, for he expects nothing beyond the visible. The closed hand betrays the eye blind to eternity.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ز ك و
أ خ ر / ك ف ر

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ لَهُمْ أَجْرٌ غَيْرُ مَمْنُونٍ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti lahum ajrun ghayru mamnūnin
versets.trad: Truly those who believe and do righteous deeds shall have a reward unceasing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds this verse in ascending degrees. “They believed”: they witnessed; “they did good”: they kept assiduous to the carpet of servanthood. They believed: they beheld the Presence; they did good: they halted at the Door. They believed: they entered; they did good: once entered, they did not turn away. And the reward “without break” is graded by ranks: for the souls, the Garden; for the hearts, contentment in God; for the spirits, intimacy with Him; for the inmost secrets, the perpetual contemplation of the Real.

mots.racine: أ م ن
mots.def: āmana: to believe, to have faith.

mots.racine: ع م ل / ص ل ح
أ ج ر / م ن ن

versets.ar: قُلْ أَئِنَّكُمْ لَتَكْفُرُونَ بِالَّذِي خَلَقَ الْأَرْضَ فِي يَوْمَيْنِ وَتَجْعَلُونَ لَهُ أَندَادًا ذَٰلِكَ رَبُّ الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: qul a-innakum la-takfurūna bi-lladhī khalaqa l-arḍa fī yawmayni wa-tajʿalūna lahū andādan dhālika rabbu l-ʿālamīna
versets.trad: Say: “Do you indeed disbelieve in Him who created the earth in two days, and do you set up equals to Him? That is the Lord of the worlds!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī meditates on the absolute priority of the Creator: He created time when there was no time before it, and created space when there was no space before it. The Real was, with neither place nor time: no place reaches Him, no time possesses Him. Hence the absurdity of equals: how could what was not, then came to be, be the peer of Him who has never ceased to be and never shall?

mots.racine: ك ف ر
خ ل ق / أ ر ض
mots.sens: created the earth in two days

mots.racine: ن د د
ر ب ب / ع ل م

versets.ar: وَجَعَلَ فِيهَا رَوَاسِيَ مِن فَوْقِهَا وَبَارَكَ فِيهَا وَقَدَّرَ فِيهَا أَقْوَاتَهَا فِي أَرْبَعَةِ أَيَّامٍ سَوَاءً لِّلسَّائِلِينَ
versets.tr: wa-jaʿala fīhā rawāsiya min fawqihā wa-bāraka fīhā wa-qaddara fīhā aqwātahā fī arbaʿati ayyāmin sawāʾan li-s-sāʾilīna
versets.trad: He set firm mountains above it, blessed it, and apportioned therein its sustenance in four days, alike for all who seek.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unveils a correspondence between the visible and the hidden: the mountains are the stakes of the earth in form, but the saints (awliyāʾ) are its stakes and anchors in reality. And blessing passes through them: rain comes to them by the blessing of the saints, and calamity is averted from them by the blessing of the saints. As for the apportioned sustenance, it is not only that of bodies: there are also nourishments for the hearts and for the inmost secrets.

mots.racine: ر س و
ب ر ك
ق د ر / ق و ت
س و ي / س أ ل

versets.ar: ثُمَّ اسْتَوَىٰ إِلَى السَّمَاءِ وَهِيَ دُخَانٌ فَقَالَ لَهَا وَلِلْأَرْضِ ائْتِيَا طَوْعًا أَوْ كَرْهًا قَالَتَا أَتَيْنَا طَائِعِينَ
versets.tr: thumma stawā ilā s-samāʾi wa-hiya dukhānun fa-qāla lahā wa-li-l-arḍi ʾtiyā ṭawʿan aw karhan qālatā ataynā ṭāʾiʿīna
versets.trad: Then He turned to the heaven while it was smoke, and said to it and to the earth: “Come, willingly or unwillingly!” They said: “We come willingly.”
versets.commentaire: “Come willingly or unwillingly”: Qushayrī first sees in it a parable of omnipotence — nothing of what He has created resists Him; of His creation He obtains all that He wills. Then he interiorizes the scene: He made the souls of the worshippers an earth for His obedience, and their hearts a firmament for the stars of His knowledge and the suns of His gnosis. The stakes of the souls are fear and hope, longing and dread; and in the hearts shine the radiance of gnosis, the suns of unity, the stars of the sciences. The hearts are in His hand: He turns them as He decrees.

mots.racine: س و ي / س م و
mots.def: istawā ilā: to direct oneself toward, to apply oneself to.

mots.racine: د خ ن
أ ت ي / ط و ع / ك ر ه
أ ت ي / ط و ع

versets.ar: فَقَضَاهُنَّ سَبْعَ سَمَاوَاتٍ فِي يَوْمَيْنِ وَأَوْحَىٰ فِي كُلِّ سَمَاءٍ أَمْرَهَا وَزَيَّنَّا السَّمَاءَ الدُّنْيَا بِمَصَابِيحَ وَحِفْظًا ذَٰلِكَ تَقْدِيرُ الْعَزِيزِ الْعَلِيمِ
versets.tr: fa-qaḍāhunna sabʿa samāwātin fī yawmayni wa-awḥā fī kulli samāʾin amrahā wa-zayyannā s-samāʾa d-dunyā bi-maṣābīḥa wa-ḥifẓan dhālika taqdīru l-ʿazīzi l-ʿalīmi
versets.trad: He completed them as seven heavens in two days and revealed to each heaven its command. We adorned the lowest heaven with lamps and a guard. Such is the ordaining of the Mighty, the All-Knowing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī transposes the adornment of heaven into the adornment of the earth of hearts: He adorned the lowest heaven with lamps, and He adorned the face of the earth with lamps that are the hearts of the beloved. When the dwellers of heaven gaze upon the hearts of the saints by night, that is their place of recreation — just as the dwellers of earth, lifting their eyes to the sky, find solace in the sight of the stars. The hearts of God's friends are thus the stars of the upper world.

mots.racine: ق ض ي / س م و
mots.sens: He completed them as seven heavens
mots.def: qaḍā: to bring to completion, to decree.

mots.racine: و ح ي / أ م ر
mots.sens: and revealed to each heaven its command

mots.racine: ص ب ح
ق د ر / ع ز ز / ع ل م
mots.sens: the ordaining of the Mighty, the Knowing

versets.ar: فَإِنْ أَعْرَضُوا فَقُلْ أَنذَرْتُكُمْ صَاعِقَةً مِّثْلَ صَاعِقَةِ عَادٍ وَثَمُودَ
versets.tr: fa-in aʿraḍū fa-qul andhartukum ṣāʿiqatan mithla ṣāʿiqati ʿādin wa-thamūda
versets.trad: If they turn away, say: “I have warned you of a thunderbolt like the thunderbolt of ʿĀd and Thamūd.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sums up the sense of the warning: tell the deniers that they have forerunners; if you walk their road of obstinacy and refuse all but stubbornness, We will join you to your likes. The ruins of the past peoples are a mirror held up to the rejecters: the same cause summons the same fate.

mots.racine: ع ر ض
ن ذ ر
ص ع ق

versets.ar: إِذْ جَاءَتْهُمُ الرُّسُلُ مِن بَيْنِ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمِنْ خَلْفِهِمْ أَلَّا تَعْبُدُوا إِلَّا اللَّهَ قَالُوا لَوْ شَاءَ رَبُّنَا لَأَنزَلَ مَلَائِكَةً فَإِنَّا بِمَا أُرْسِلْتُم بِهِ كَافِرُونَ
versets.tr: idh jāʾathumu r-rusulu min bayni aydīhim wa-min khalfihim allā taʿbudū illā llāha qālū law shāʾa rabbunā la-anzala malāʾikatan fa-innā bi-mā ursiltum bihī kāfirūna
versets.trad: When the messengers came to them from before them and behind them, saying: “Worship none but God,” they replied: “Had our Lord willed, He would have sent down angels; so we disbelieve in that with which you are sent.”
versets.commentaire: The messengers press them “from before and behind”: from every side the call envelops them, leaving no escape but willful blindness. Their response — demanding angels rather than men — reveals, in Qushayrī's reading, the heart of denial: they measure the messenger by the gauge of their carnal expectation, refusing that grace should come through one like themselves. They thus deny not the message but its path of humility.

mots.racine: ج ي أ / ر س ل
ي د ي / خ ل ف
ع ب د
ر س ل / ك ف ر

versets.ar: فَأَمَّا عَادٌ فَاسْتَكْبَرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ بِغَيْرِ الْحَقِّ وَقَالُوا مَنْ أَشَدُّ مِنَّا قُوَّةً أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا أَنَّ اللَّهَ الَّذِي خَلَقَهُمْ هُوَ أَشَدُّ مِنْهُمْ قُوَّةً وَكَانُوا بِآيَاتِنَا يَجْحَدُونَ
versets.tr: fa-ammā ʿādun fa-stakbarū fī l-arḍi bi-ghayri l-ḥaqqi wa-qālū man ashaddu minnā quwwatan a-wa-lam yaraw anna llāha lladhī khalaqahum huwa ashaddu minhum quwwatan wa-kānū bi-āyātinā yajḥadūna
versets.trad: As for ʿĀd, they grew arrogant on the earth without right and said: “Who is mightier than us in strength?” Did they not see that God, who created them, is mightier than they in strength? Yet they kept denying Our signs.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī condenses the fate of ʿĀd into a maxim on self-reliance: they leaned on the strength of their own souls, and their strength betrayed them; their affliction took hold of them. Whoever thinks himself strong through himself discovers that his strength is precisely the site of his ruin, for he has turned it against his Creator, who is mightier than he.

mots.racine: ك ب ر / أ ر ض
mots.sens: they grew arrogant on the earth

mots.racine: ش د د / ق و ي
mots.sens: who is mightier than us in strength

mots.racine: ش د د / ق و ي
mots.def: the superiority of the divine power.

mots.racine: أ ي ي / ج ح د

versets.ar: فَأَرْسَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ رِيحًا صَرْصَرًا فِي أَيَّامٍ نَّحِسَاتٍ لِّنُذِيقَهُمْ عَذَابَ الْخِزْيِ فِي الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا وَلَعَذَابُ الْآخِرَةِ أَخْزَىٰ وَهُمْ لَا يُنصَرُونَ
versets.tr: fa-arsalnā ʿalayhim rīḥan ṣarṣaran fī ayyāmin naḥisātin li-nudhīqahum ʿadhāba l-khizyi fī l-ḥayāti d-dunyā wa-la-ʿadhābu l-ākhirati akhzā wa-hum lā yunṣarūna
versets.trad: So We sent against them a howling wind during days of misfortune, to make them taste the punishment of disgrace in the life of this world; but the punishment of the Hereafter is more disgracing, and they will not be helped.
versets.commentaire: The icy, howling wind sweeps away ʿĀd: Qushayrī recalls that it left not one of them. The punishment of this world is but a foretaste: that of the Hereafter is more abasing still. Thus the proud one who thought himself invincible is swept off by a breath, that it may blaze forth how created strength is nothing before the command of the Creator.

mots.racine: ر و ح / ص ر ص ر
ن ح س
ع ذ ب / خ ز ي
ن ص ر

versets.ar: وَأَمَّا ثَمُودُ فَهَدَيْنَاهُمْ فَاسْتَحَبُّوا الْعَمَىٰ عَلَى الْهُدَىٰ فَأَخَذَتْهُمْ صَاعِقَةُ الْعَذَابِ الْهُونِ بِمَا كَانُوا يَكْسِبُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ammā thamūdu fa-hadaynāhum fa-staḥabbū l-ʿamā ʿalā l-hudā fa-akhadhathum ṣāʿiqatu l-ʿadhābi l-hūni bi-mā kānū yaksibūna
versets.trad: As for Thamūd, We guided them, but they preferred blindness to guidance; so the thunderbolt of humiliating punishment seized them for what they had earned.
versets.commentaire: Thamūd received guidance, then “preferred blindness to guidance.” Qushayrī reports that they believed and assented at first, then apostatized and denied: so God made them follow the way of their brethren in extermination. To receive the light and choose blindness is a graver fault than never to have seen: it is to scorn the gift after having held it.

mots.racine: ه د ي
mots.def: hadā: to guide, to show the way.

mots.racine: ح ب ب / ع م ي / ه د ي
ص ع ق / ع ذ ب / ه و ن
ك س ب

versets.ar: وَنَجَّيْنَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَكَانُوا يَتَّقُونَ
versets.tr: wa-najjaynā lladhīna āmanū wa-kānū yattaqūna
versets.trad: And We saved those who believed and were godfearing.
versets.commentaire: On salvation, Qushayrī unfolds the degrees of those whom God delivers: some are saved without even seeing the Fire, crossing the bridge unawares; others pass like the flashing lightning — these are the highest; others like the galloping horseman, also among the great; others stumble on the Ṣirāṭ and the angels set them upright again. And even for those whom the Fire touches a moment, when the flame reaches the heart, the Real says to it: “Burn not his heart, for it has burned in Me.” Godfearing piety is the rope that holds one back from the brink.

mots.racine: ن ج و
أ م ن
و ق ي
mots.def: ittaqā: to guard oneself through piety.

versets.ar: وَيَوْمَ يُحْشَرُ أَعْدَاءُ اللَّهِ إِلَى النَّارِ فَهُمْ يُوزَعُونَ
versets.tr: wa-yawma yuḥsharu aʿdāʾu llāhi ilā n-nāri fa-hum yūzaʿūna
versets.trad: And on the Day when the enemies of God are gathered toward the Fire, they will be driven in ranks.
versets.commentaire: “The enemies of God” are led to the Fire “in ranks”: Qushayrī meditates on this dreadful name — to be called the enemy of God is the utmost degradation, whereas the friend (walī) is led, for his part, to nearness. The ordered gathering, herded in files, marks that at the hour of judgment none flees the decree: each is driven toward the place his deeds have prepared.

mots.racine: ح ش ر
ع د و
و ز ع
mots.def: wazaʿa: to restrain, to marshal in groups.

versets.ar: حَتَّىٰ إِذَا مَا جَاءُوهَا شَهِدَ عَلَيْهِمْ سَمْعُهُمْ وَأَبْصَارُهُمْ وَجُلُودُهُم بِمَا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: ḥattā idhā mā jāʾūhā shahida ʿalayhim samʿuhum wa-abṣāruhum wa-julūduhum bi-mā kānū yaʿmalūna
versets.trad: Until, when they reach it, their hearing, their sight and their skins bear witness against them as to what they used to do.
versets.commentaire: The very limbs of the sinner rise as witnesses. Qushayrī underlines the reversal: their own parts testified against them, though they had never suspected that God would make them speak — He who has given speech to all things; and it never crossed their minds what painful destiny awaited them. What was complicit in the transgression becomes the instrument of the sentence.

mots.racine: ج ي أ
ش ه د
س م ع / ب ص ر / ج ل د

versets.ar: وَقَالُوا لِجُلُودِهِمْ لِمَ شَهِدتُّمْ عَلَيْنَا قَالُوا أَنطَقَنَا اللَّهُ الَّذِي أَنطَقَ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ وَهُوَ خَلَقَكُمْ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ وَإِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qālū li-julūdihim lima shahidtum ʿalaynā qālū anṭaqanā llāhu lladhī anṭaqa kulla shayʾin wa-huwa khalaqakum awwala marratin wa-ilayhi turjaʿūna
versets.trad: They will say to their skins: “Why did you bear witness against us?” They will answer: “God made us speak, He who has given speech to all things, who created you the first time, and to Him you are returned.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī pauses on “He who has given speech to all things”: if God makes the mute skin speak, it is because no creature has a voice of its own — every utterance is a gift, lent by Him who animates inert matter. The same power that brought man into being “the first time” brings him back; and whoever can listen discovers that the whole universe, even now, already bears witness to its Lord.

mots.racine: ش ه د
mots.sens: why did you testify against us

mots.racine: ن ط ق
ن ط ق
ر ج ع

versets.ar: وَمَا كُنتُمْ تَسْتَتِرُونَ أَن يَشْهَدَ عَلَيْكُمْ سَمْعُكُمْ وَلَا أَبْصَارُكُمْ وَلَا جُلُودُكُمْ وَلَٰكِن ظَنَنتُمْ أَنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَعْلَمُ كَثِيرًا مِّمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā kuntum tastatirūna an yashhada ʿalaykum samʿukum wa-lā abṣārukum wa-lā julūdukum wa-lākin ẓanantum anna llāha lā yaʿlamu kathīran mimmā taʿmalūna
versets.trad: You did not conceal yourselves lest your hearing, your sight or your skins bear witness against you; rather you supposed that God does not know much of what you do.
versets.commentaire: Their ruin springs from a false opinion held about God: to believe He is unaware of part of their deeds. Qushayrī warns that the opinion held of God, when it is beautiful, is met with realization; but when it is born of illusion and is not sanctioned by the Law, it ruins the one who nurses it. To think the divine gaze has blind spots is to deliver oneself to one's own undoing.

mots.racine: س ت ر
ش ه د
ظ ن ن
ع ل م / ع م ل
mots.sens: does not know much of your deeds
mots.def: false belief in a limited knowledge.

versets.ar: وَذَٰلِكُمْ ظَنُّكُمُ الَّذِي ظَنَنتُم بِرَبِّكُمْ أَرْدَاكُمْ فَأَصْبَحْتُم مِّنَ الْخَاسِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-dhālikum ẓannukumu lladhī ẓanantum bi-rabbikum ardākum fa-aṣbaḥtum mina l-khāsirīna
versets.trad: And that thought of yours which you held about your Lord has ruined you, and you have become among the losers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of this verse a law of the heart. The beautiful opinion held of God — by my life — is repaid with realization; but the opinion born of pride and unsanctioned by the Law destroys its author. He also aims at one who poses as a master through words without purity of state: nothing is accepted from him save on proof of his state; if the state belies the saying, one no longer leans on him. Inner thought shapes destiny.

mots.racine: ظ ن ن / ر ب ب
ر د ي
خ س ر

versets.ar: فَإِن يَصْبِرُوا فَالنَّارُ مَثْوًى لَّهُمْ وَإِن يَسْتَعْتِبُوا فَمَا هُم مِّنَ الْمُعْتَبِينَ
versets.tr: fa-in yaṣbirū fa-n-nāru mathwan lahum wa-in yastaʿtibū fa-mā hum mina l-muʿtabīna
versets.trad: If they endure, the Fire is their dwelling; and if they seek favor, they are not among those granted favor.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī bolts every door of escape: if they endure on the site of their engulfing, they will tumble into the Fire; and if they implore that their excuse be accepted, their excuse will not be accepted. The Fire is their dwelling in either case, with no possible flight: neither patience nor belated supplication avails — the hour of grace has passed.

mots.racine: ص ب ر
ن و ر / ث و ي
ع ت ب
ع ت ب

versets.ar: وَقَيَّضْنَا لَهُمْ قُرَنَاءَ فَزَيَّنُوا لَهُم مَّا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ وَحَقَّ عَلَيْهِمُ الْقَوْلُ فِي أُمَمٍ قَدْ خَلَتْ مِن قَبْلِهِم مِّنَ الْجِنِّ وَالْإِنسِ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا خَاسِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-qayyaḍnā lahum quranāʾa fa-zayyanū lahum mā bayna aydīhim wa-mā khalfahum wa-ḥaqqa ʿalayhimu l-qawlu fī umamin qad khalat min qablihim mina l-jinni wa-l-insi innahum kānū khāsirīna
versets.trad: We assigned to them companions who made fair to them what was before them and behind them; and the word fell due against them, along with communities of jinn and men that had passed away before them. Indeed, they were losers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī opens here a spiritual psychology of companions. When God wills good for a servant, He assigns him good companions who help him to obedience, urge him to it, and summon him to it; but if they are companions of evil, they push him to transgression. And he names the worst of all: the devil is posted against man to whisper evil to him — but worse still is the soul (nafs), an evil companion: today it calls the servant to his perdition, tomorrow it will testify against him of his fault. What is made fair “before them” is long hope; “behind them,” forgetfulness of the fault and the deferral of repentance.

mots.racine: ق ي ض / ق ر ن
mots.def: qayyaḍa: to assign; qarīn: inseparable companion.

mots.racine: ز ي ن
ح ق ق / ق و ل
mots.sens: the word fell due against them
mots.def: ḥaqqa: to become due, to come true.

mots.racine: ج ن ن / أ ن س

versets.ar: وَقَالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَا تَسْمَعُوا لِهَٰذَا الْقُرْآنِ وَالْغَوْا فِيهِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَغْلِبُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qāla lladhīna kafarū lā tasmaʿū li-hādhā l-qurʾāni wa-lghaw fīhi laʿallakum taghlibūna
versets.trad: The disbelievers say: “Do not listen to this Qurʾān, but make noise over it, that perhaps you may prevail.”
themes: the conquering power of the Word
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī pierces the motive of their clamor: denial reigned over their hearts and obstinacy persisted in their hostility; they schemed by every means and counseled one another not to listen to this Qurʾān — for it overpowers hearts, ravishes minds, and whoever hears it inclines toward it. So they advised drowning its recitation in noise. But they did not know that he whose heart God has illumined with faith, supported with understanding, and to whom He has unveiled the hearing of the secret from the Unseen — he alone hears and believes; whereas he who festers in the darkness of his ignorance, faith does not enter his heart, nor does the hearing touch his secret.

mots.racine: س م ع / ق ر أ
mots.sens: do not listen to this Qurʾān

mots.racine: ل غ و
mots.def: laghā: to speak idly, to drown out.

mots.racine: غ ل ب

versets.ar: فَلَنُذِيقَنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا عَذَابًا شَدِيدًا وَلَنَجْزِيَنَّهُمْ أَسْوَأَ الَّذِي كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: fa-la-nudhīqanna lladhīna kafarū ʿadhāban shadīdan wa-la-najziyannahum aswaʾa lladhī kānū yaʿmalūna
versets.trad: We will surely make the disbelievers taste a severe punishment, and We will requite them for the worst of what they used to do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes two times of punishment: today through perpetual deprivation, which is separation; and tomorrow through eternity in the Fire, which is the burning. The worst of their deeds is returned to them — but for the knower, the keenest torment is not the flame: it is the firāq, the exile far from the Face. The present deprivation already prefigures the eternal rupture.

mots.racine: ذ و ق
ع ذ ب / ش د د
ج ز ي
س و أ / ع م ل
mots.sens: the worst of what they did

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ جَزَاءُ أَعْدَاءِ اللَّهِ النَّارُ لَهُمْ فِيهَا دَارُ الْخُلْدِ جَزَاءً بِمَا كَانُوا بِآيَاتِنَا يَجْحَدُونَ
versets.tr: dhālika jazāʾu aʿdāʾi llāhi n-nāru lahum fīhā dāru l-khuldi jazāʾan bi-mā kānū bi-āyātinā yajḥadūna
versets.trad: That is the recompense of the enemies of God: the Fire. Therein they have the abode of eternity, a recompense for having denied Our signs.
versets.commentaire: “The abode of eternity”: Qushayrī describes it in a word — therein they find disgrace and humiliation, without break or end. The perpetuity of the punishment answers the obstinacy of the denial: for having denied His signs without respite, the rejecter undergoes an abasement without respite. Where the friend knows the duration of union, the enemy knows the duration of exile.

mots.racine: ج ز ي / ع د و / ن و ر
mots.sens: the recompense of God's enemies: the Fire

mots.racine: د و ر / خ ل د
أ ي ي / ج ح د

versets.ar: وَقَالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا رَبَّنَا أَرِنَا اللَّذَيْنِ أَضَلَّانَا مِنَ الْجِنِّ وَالْإِنسِ نَجْعَلْهُمَا تَحْتَ أَقْدَامِنَا لِيَكُونَا مِنَ الْأَسْفَلِينَ
versets.tr: wa-qāla lladhīna kafarū rabbanā arinā lladhayni aḍallānā mina l-jinni wa-l-insi najʿalhumā taḥta aqdāminā li-yakūnā mina l-asfalīna
versets.trad: The disbelievers will say: “Our Lord, show us the two who led us astray, among jinn and men; we will put them beneath our feet, that they may be among the lowest.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī identifies the two who led astray: among the jinn, Iblīs; among men, Cain son of Adam, the first to institute disobedience when he slew his brother. And he unveils the futility of the wish: this desire to trample them underfoot is but an increase of their own torment, for they distress themselves over it without profit — their wish will not be granted and the punishment will not be lifted. The verse shows the mutual disavowal of accomplices, and that regret out of its time avails nothing.

mots.racine: ر أ ي / ض ل ل
mots.sens: show us the two who led us astray

mots.racine: ج ع ل / ق د م
mots.sens: we will put them beneath our feet

mots.racine: س ف ل
mots.sens: that they be among the lowest

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ قَالُوا رَبُّنَا اللَّهُ ثُمَّ اسْتَقَامُوا تَتَنَزَّلُ عَلَيْهِمُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ أَلَّا تَخَافُوا وَلَا تَحْزَنُوا وَأَبْشِرُوا بِالْجَنَّةِ الَّتِي كُنتُمْ تُوعَدُونَ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna qālū rabbunā llāhu thumma staqāmū tatanazzalu ʿalayhimu l-malāʾikatu allā takhāfū wa-lā taḥzanū wa-abshirū bi-l-jannati llatī kuntum tūʿadūna
versets.trad: Truly those who say: “Our Lord is God,” then stand firm — the angels descend upon them: “Fear not, nor grieve, but rejoice in the Garden that you were promised.”
versets.commentaire: This is the heart of the sūra, and Qushayrī pours out here his most beautiful allusions on istiqāma. Uprightness is firmness upon all the conditions of faith, without falling short in any of its parts. He grades its faces: the uprightness of the renunciant: not to return to the world nor be diverted from God by rank; the uprightness of the knower: that his gnosis be unmixed with any share in the two abodes, which would veil him from his Master; the uprightness of the worshipper: not to relapse into lukewarmness nor ostentation; the uprightness of the lover: to have no desire but the Beloved, contenting himself with His permanence for every gift. And he interprets the angels' consolation: “Fear not” — for fear is born of expecting a future ill or losing a beloved; “grieve not” — for sorrow comes from the bitterness of the moment, and whoever is content with the decree has no grief. Thus he who abides in contentment lives, even here below, in repose.

mots.racine: ق و ل / ر ب ب
mots.sens: they said: our Lord is God

mots.racine: ق و م
ن ز ل / م ل ك
خ و ف / ح ز ن
mots.def: khawf: fear of the future; ḥuzn: sorrow over the past.

mots.racine: ب ش ر / ج ن ن

versets.ar: نَحْنُ أَوْلِيَاؤُكُمْ فِي الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ وَلَكُمْ فِيهَا مَا تَشْتَهِي أَنفُسُكُمْ وَلَكُمْ فِيهَا مَا تَدَّعُونَ
versets.tr: naḥnu awliyāʾukum fī l-ḥayāti d-dunyā wa-fī l-ākhirati wa-lakum fīhā mā tashtahī anfusukum wa-lakum fīhā mā taddaʿūna
versets.trad: We are your protectors in the life of this world and in the Hereafter. Therein you shall have whatever your souls desire, and therein you shall have whatever you call for,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the twofold walāya by degrees. “We are your friends here below” by the realization of gnosis, “and in the Hereafter” by the granting of forgiveness; here below by contemplation, there by face-to-face vision; here below by contentment with the decree, there by the meeting in the abode of permanence; here below by love, there by nearness. And he gives a pearl on the precedence of walāya over its very fruits: walāya is cash, the obtaining of desires is a promise — whoever busies himself with the cash has little care for the promise.

mots.racine: و ل ي
ح ي ي / د ن و / أ خ ر
mots.sens: in this world and the next

mots.racine: ش ه و / ن ف س
د ع و

versets.ar: نُزُلًا مِّنْ غَفُورٍ رَّحِيمٍ
versets.tr: nuzulan min ghafūrin raḥīmin
versets.trad: a welcome from One Forgiving, Compassionate.
versets.commentaire: “A welcome-gift” (nuzul): Qushayrī sees in it a grace and a present, the firstfruits of those favors and bounties that will last forever. And he softens at the two Names that seal the promise: “from One Forgiving, Compassionate” — there is the space opened to the hopes of sinners, for it is they who need forgiveness; and were it not for His mercy, they would never reach His pardon.

mots.racine: ن ز ل
mots.def: nuzul: what is offered to the arriving guest.

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ر ح م

versets.ar: وَمَنْ أَحْسَنُ قَوْلًا مِّمَّن دَعَا إِلَى اللَّهِ وَعَمِلَ صَالِحًا وَقَالَ إِنَّنِي مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-man aḥsanu qawlan mimman daʿā ilā llāhi wa-ʿamila ṣāliḥan wa-qāla innanī mina l-muslimīna
versets.trad: Who is fairer in speech than one who calls to God, does righteousness, and says: “Truly I am of those who submit”?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī elevates the definition of the one who calls to God: he is the one who calls people to suffice themselves with God and to abandon the quest for a reward from God; he commits his affair to God and is content with the portion God allots him. And he joins the call to the act: just as he calls creatures to God, he himself performs what he calls them to. Better still: they first traversed the path of God, and by their wayfaring and their halts they came to know the way to Him, then they invited creatures to it. As for the “submitters” (muslimūn), they are those who are content with His decree and His measure.

mots.racine: ح س ن / ق و ل
د ع و
mots.sens: than one who calls to God

mots.racine: ع م ل / ص ل ح
س ل م
mots.def: muslim: one who surrenders to God.

versets.ar: وَلَا تَسْتَوِي الْحَسَنَةُ وَلَا السَّيِّئَةُ ادْفَعْ بِالَّتِي هِيَ أَحْسَنُ فَإِذَا الَّذِي بَيْنَكَ وَبَيْنَهُ عَدَاوَةٌ كَأَنَّهُ وَلِيٌّ حَمِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-lā tastawī l-ḥasanatu wa-lā s-sayyiʾatu dfaʿ bi-llatī hiya aḥsanu fa-idhā lladhī baynaka wa-baynahū ʿadāwatun kaʾannahū waliyyun ḥamīmun
versets.trad: The good deed and the evil deed are not equal. Repel with that which is better, and behold: the one between whom and you was enmity becomes as a warm friend.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of this verse a lesson in futuwwa, spiritual chivalry. To repel with what is better is pardon instead of retaliation, indulgence and forbearance before the fault, the renouncing of revenge. And the enmity turned to warmth has a higher root: this belongs to the beauty of conduct in service; because of your companionship with God, you show clemency toward His servants for His sake. Beauty of character toward creatures is the reverse side of fidelity to the Creator — not to take revenge for oneself, and to pardon one's adversary.

mots.racine: س و ي / ح س ن / س و أ
mots.sens: the good and the evil are not equal
mots.def: ḥasana: good deed; sayyiʾa: evil deed.

mots.racine: د ف ع / ح س ن
mots.def: dafaʿa: to repel, to ward off.

mots.racine: ع د و
و ل ي / ح م م

versets.ar: وَمَا يُلَقَّاهَا إِلَّا الَّذِينَ صَبَرُوا وَمَا يُلَقَّاهَا إِلَّا ذُو حَظٍّ عَظِيمٍ
versets.tr: wa-mā yulaqqāhā illā lladhīna ṣabarū wa-mā yulaqqāhā illā dhū ḥaẓẓin ʿaẓīmin
versets.trad: But none attains it save those who are patient; none attains it save the possessor of a mighty portion.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reserves this summit of character for the steadfast: none discharges the right of these virtues save one whom the grace of patience has honored, and who has been raised from base inclinations to the heights of noble character; and none reaches the fairest of degrees save one who has been patient in the trial of hardships. The “mighty portion” is no merit wrested but a gift: patience itself is a favor deposited in the heart.

mots.racine: ل ق ي
mots.def: luqqiya: to receive, to be granted.

mots.racine: ص ب ر
ح ظ ظ / ع ظ م

versets.ar: وَإِمَّا يَنزَغَنَّكَ مِنَ الشَّيْطَانِ نَزْغٌ فَاسْتَعِذْ بِاللَّهِ إِنَّهُ هُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ
versets.tr: wa-immā yanzaghannaka mina sh-shayṭāni nazghun fa-staʿidh bi-llāhi innahū huwa s-samīʿu l-ʿalīmu
versets.trad: And if an incitement from Satan provokes you, seek refuge in God; He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī describes the insidious slope of temptation: if the whisperings of the devil touch you, hasten to the remembrance of your Lord and return to Him before the first step. For if you do not oppose the first fleeting thought, it becomes a fixed idea, then a resolve; failing to seize it, the fault is accomplished, and failing a fair return, it becomes transgression — and with time you find yourself exposed to every affliction. The only deliverance is the sincerity of recourse: the more the servant disavows his own power and strength, the more he purifies himself before God by his pleading and refuge, the more God redoubles His protection and repels the devil far from him.

mots.racine: ن ز غ
mots.def: nazagha: to goad to evil, to sow discord.

mots.racine: ن ز غ
ع و ذ
س م ع / ع ل م
mots.sens: He is the Hearing, the Knowing

versets.ar: وَمِنْ آيَاتِهِ اللَّيْلُ وَالنَّهَارُ وَالشَّمْسُ وَالْقَمَرُ لَا تَسْجُدُوا لِلشَّمْسِ وَلَا لِلْقَمَرِ وَاسْجُدُوا لِلَّهِ الَّذِي خَلَقَهُنَّ إِن كُنتُمْ إِيَّاهُ تَعْبُدُونَ
versets.tr: wa-min āyātihi l-laylu wa-n-nahāru wa-sh-shamsu wa-l-qamaru lā tasjudū li-sh-shamsi wa-lā li-l-qamari wa-sjudū li-llāhi lladhī khalaqahunna in kuntum iyyāhu taʿbudūna
versets.trad: Among His signs are the night and the day, the sun and the moon. Do not prostrate to the sun nor to the moon, but prostrate to God who created them, if it is truly Him you worship.
themes: the signs of the heavenly bodies
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī hears in this prohibition a jealousy of love: “Do not prostrate to the sun” in its splendor, “nor to the moon” in its brightness; “prostrate to God” — for He felt jealousy at seeing you prostrate to another. And he reverses the perspective: the sun — however high — and the moon — however lovely — it is for you that We created them; do not prostrate to them, prostrate to Us. Then he presses the conscience: the Real commands you to guard your face from bowing to sun and moon — yet you, for the meanest paltry gain, carry your steps to anyone and enter with your face upon anyone!

mots.racine: أ ي ي
ل ي ل / ن ه ر / ش م س / ق م ر
س ج د
س ج د / خ ل ق
mots.sens: prostrate to God who created them

versets.ar: فَإِنِ اسْتَكْبَرُوا فَالَّذِينَ عِندَ رَبِّكَ يُسَبِّحُونَ لَهُ بِاللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ وَهُمْ لَا يَسْأَمُونَ
versets.tr: fa-ini stakbarū fa-lladhīna ʿinda rabbika yusabbiḥūna lahū bi-l-layli wa-n-nahāri wa-hum lā yasʾamūna
versets.trad: But if they grow arrogant, those who are with your Lord glorify Him night and day, and they never weary.
themes: the tireless praise of the angels
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī illumines the divine self-sufficiency: if the disbelievers swell with pride, no breach results, for the Real has no need of anyone. Then he opposes to their weariness the fervor of the angels: those, dwellers of the last abode, prostrate to Him night and day, never tiring of His worship. The arrogant turns away and thinks he harms; but the whole heaven praises without cease: his refusal subtracts nothing from the glory of the Praised.

mots.racine: ك ب ر
ر ب ب
mots.sens: those who are with your Lord

mots.racine: س ب ح
mots.def: sabbaḥa: to extol, to proclaim transcendence.

mots.racine: س أ م
mots.def: saʾima: to tire, to grow weary.

versets.ar: وَمِنْ آيَاتِهِ أَنَّكَ تَرَى الْأَرْضَ خَاشِعَةً فَإِذَا أَنزَلْنَا عَلَيْهَا الْمَاءَ اهْتَزَّتْ وَرَبَتْ إِنَّ الَّذِي أَحْيَاهَا لَمُحْيِ الْمَوْتَىٰ إِنَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: wa-min āyātihi annaka tarā l-arḍa khāshiʿatan fa-idhā anzalnā ʿalayhā l-māʾa htazzat wa-rabat inna lladhī aḥyāhā la-muḥyi l-mawtā innahū ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīrun
versets.trad: Among His signs is that you see the earth lowly and barren; but when We send down water upon it, it stirs and swells. Truly He who gives it life is the giver of life to the dead. He has power over all things.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī transposes the resurrection of the earth into the resurrection of hearts. The earth is arid and dead in winter; when the rain descends, it quivers with greenery. So too the hearts: when they grow humble (khāshiʿa) at feeling the wound of sins, the Real advances toward them, and there appear in them the blessings of repentance. He extends the image to the wayfarer's lukewarmness: when a time of languor befalls the servant, an absence far from the carpet of obedience, then the Real covers him with remembrance — lights of accord rise in the heart, he returns to his accustomed station, and the tree of his concord, after the drought, blooms again under the water of solicitude. And Qushayrī concludes: He who gave the earth life is able to revive the souls by the gathering, and to revive the hearts by the light of grace after failing and veiling.

mots.racine: ر أ ي / أ ر ض / خ ش ع
mots.sens: you see the earth humble and dead

mots.racine: ن ز ل / م و ه
mots.sens: We send down water upon it

mots.racine: ه ز ز / ر ب و
mots.def: ihtazza: to quiver; rabā: to grow.

mots.racine: ح ي ي / م و ت

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُلْحِدُونَ فِي آيَاتِنَا لَا يَخْفَوْنَ عَلَيْنَا أَفَمَن يُلْقَىٰ فِي النَّارِ خَيْرٌ أَم مَّن يَأْتِي آمِنًا يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ اعْمَلُوا مَا شِئْتُمْ إِنَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna yulḥidūna fī āyātinā lā yakhfawna ʿalaynā a-fa-man yulqā fī n-nāri khayrun am man yaʾtī āminan yawma l-qiyāmati ʿmalū mā shiʾtum innahū bi-mā taʿmalūna baṣīrun
versets.trad: Those who distort Our signs are not hidden from Us. Is he who is cast into the Fire better, or he who comes secure on the Day of Resurrection? Do what you will; He sees clearly what you do.
versets.commentaire: “Those who deviate concerning Our signs do not escape Us.” Qushayrī reads the “Do what you will” as a veiled threat rather than a permission: they will receive the punishment they deserve; let them do what they will — they run only to their own blame, they walk only toward their perdition, on their own feet. The freedom left to the obstinate is no grace but a rope: each act tightens the noose he has slipped around his own neck.

mots.racine: ل ح د / أ ي ي
خ ف ي
ل ق ي / ن و ر
mots.sens: he who is cast into the Fire

mots.racine: ع م ل / ش ي أ
mots.def: threat in the form of permission.

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِالذِّكْرِ لَمَّا جَاءَهُمْ وَإِنَّهُ لَكِتَابٌ عَزِيزٌ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna kafarū bi-dh-dhikri lammā jāʾahum wa-innahū la-kitābun ʿazīzun
versets.trad: Those who disbelieve in the Reminder when it comes to them — and indeed it is a mighty Book —
versets.commentaire: On “a mighty Book” (ʿazīz), Qushayrī unfolds all the senses of the word. A Book mighty and without peer, before which they were powerless to produce its like; a triumphant Book, which overthrows the sophistries of innovators and renegades; a Book none can contradict. It is the Word of a mighty Lord, to a mighty Messenger, by the embassy of a mighty angel, to a mighty community. And he unveils its most tender sense: it is dear (ʿazīz) to the believers, for it is the book of their Beloved — and the letter of the Beloved to the beloved is precious.

mots.racine: ك ف ر / ذ ك ر
ج ي أ
mots.def: at the very moment of its coming.

mots.racine: ك ت ب / ع ز ز
mots.sens: it is a mighty, dear Book

versets.ar: لَّا يَأْتِيهِ الْبَاطِلُ مِن بَيْنِ يَدَيْهِ وَلَا مِنْ خَلْفِهِ تَنزِيلٌ مِّنْ حَكِيمٍ حَمِيدٍ
versets.tr: lā yaʾtīhi l-bāṭilu min bayni yadayhi wa-lā min khalfihī tanzīlun min ḥakīmin ḥamīdin
versets.trad: Falsehood cannot reach it, from before it or from behind it; it is a sending-down from One Wise, Praiseworthy.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the sense of this inviolability: no other book abrogates it, neither those that preceded it nor those that might come after — there is no book after it, nor any abrogation of it. And he adds an inner harmony: its meaning does not repel its wording, nor does its wording belie its meaning. Sent down from “One Wise, Praised,” it bears in itself the perfect accord of word and truth: neither past nor future can introduce falsehood into it.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ب ط ل

mots.ar: مِن بَيْنِ يَدَيْهِ وَلَا مِنْ خَلْفِهِ
mots.tr: min bayni yadayhi wa-lā min khalfihī
mots.racine: ي د ي / خ ل ف
mots.sens: from before it nor behind it

mots.racine: ن ز ل / ح ك م / ح م د
mots.sens: a sending-down from One Wise, Praised

versets.ar: مَّا يُقَالُ لَكَ إِلَّا مَا قَدْ قِيلَ لِلرُّسُلِ مِن قَبْلِكَ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَذُو مَغْفِرَةٍ وَذُو عِقَابٍ أَلِيمٍ
versets.tr: mā yuqālu laka illā mā qad qīla li-r-rusuli min qablika inna rabbaka la-dhū maghfiratin wa-dhū ʿiqābin alīmin
versets.trad: Nothing is said to you but what was said to the messengers before you. Truly your Lord is the Master of forgiveness and the Master of a painful punishment.
themes: the unity of the prophetic message
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī here grounds the unity of revelation: the principles of unity do not vary with the Laws; their substance, in their rulings, is one — to obey His commands, to shun His prohibitions. And the twofold face of the Lord — forgiveness and punishment — runs through all the Scriptures: in every Book and for every community, God has made known that He rewards the obedient and reserves for the deniers a harsh torment. The comfort to the Prophet is thus a reminder: opposition is nothing new, and the Master of forgiveness always precedes the Master of punishment.

mots.racine: ق و ل
ق و ل / ر س ل
mots.sens: save what was said to the messengers

mots.racine: غ ف ر
ع ق ب / أ ل م

versets.ar: وَلَوْ جَعَلْنَاهُ قُرْآنًا أَعْجَمِيًّا لَّقَالُوا لَوْلَا فُصِّلَتْ آيَاتُهُ أَأَعْجَمِيٌّ وَعَرَبِيٌّ قُلْ هُوَ لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا هُدًى وَشِفَاءٌ وَالَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ فِي آذَانِهِمْ وَقْرٌ وَهُوَ عَلَيْهِمْ عَمًى أُولَٰئِكَ يُنَادَوْنَ مِن مَّكَانٍ بَعِيدٍ
versets.tr: wa-law jaʿalnāhu qurʾānan aʿjamiyyan la-qālū lawlā fuṣṣilat āyātuhū a-aʿjamiyyun wa-ʿarabiyyun qul huwa li-lladhīna āmanū hudan wa-shifāʾun wa-lladhīna lā yuʾminūna fī ādhānihim waqrun wa-huwa ʿalayhim ʿaman ulāʾika yunādawna min makānin baʿīdin
versets.trad: Had We made it a foreign Qurʾān, they would have said: “If only its verses were expounded! A foreign tongue and an Arab?” Say: “For those who believe it is guidance and healing; but those who do not believe — in their ears is deafness, and it is blindness upon them. They are called from a distant place.”
themes: the Book a veil for the unbeliever
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the degrees of the healing the Qurʾān works. It is healing for the scholars, who rest in it from the toil of reflection and the bewilderment of thoughts; healing for the straitness of the breasts of the aspirants, through the delight of its recitation and the savor of its meditation; healing for the hearts of the lovers from the pangs of longing, through the subtlety of the ecstatic states it carries; healing for the hearts of the knowers, through what pours upon them of the lights of realization and the traces of the discourse of the mighty Lord. But for the deaf of heart, it remains blindness: they do not listen to the Real and do not respond, abiding in the darkness of denial and ignorance.

mots.racine: ق ر أ / ع ج م
ف ص ل
mots.sens: if only its verses were expounded

mots.racine: ه د ي / ش ف ي
ع م ي

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنَا مُوسَى الْكِتَابَ فَاخْتُلِفَ فِيهِ وَلَوْلَا كَلِمَةٌ سَبَقَتْ مِن رَّبِّكَ لَقُضِيَ بَيْنَهُمْ وَإِنَّهُمْ لَفِي شَكٍّ مِّنْهُ مُرِيبٍ
versets.tr: wa-laqad ātaynā mūsā l-kitāba fa-khtulifa fīhi wa-lawlā kalimatun sabaqat min rabbika la-quḍiya baynahum wa-innahum la-fī shakkin minhu murībin
versets.trad: We gave Moses the Book, and it was disputed about. Were it not for a word that preceded from your Lord, it would have been judged between them; for they are in disquieting doubt about it.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī illumines the divergence by the partition of hearts: We gave Moses the Torah and sent him to his people; they differed about it: the one whose secret We adorned with the light of unity believed it, and the one We blinded to the places of evidence belied it and denied it. As for the delay, it is a “word already pronounced”: were it not for the sentence fixing their punishment in the Fire after the Resurrection, We would have hastened their extermination and made them taste their evil fate at once.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ك ت ب
خ ل ف
ك ل م / س ب ق
mots.sens: a word already passed from your Lord
mots.def: the prior decree of the delay.

mots.racine: ش ك ك / ر ي ب

versets.ar: مَّنْ عَمِلَ صَالِحًا فَلِنَفْسِهِ وَمَنْ أَسَاءَ فَعَلَيْهَا وَمَا رَبُّكَ بِظَلَّامٍ لِّلْعَبِيدِ
versets.tr: man ʿamila ṣāliḥan fa-li-nafsihī wa-man asāʾa fa-ʿalayhā wa-mā rabbuka bi-ẓallāmin li-l-ʿabīdi
versets.trad: Whoever does righteousness, it is for himself; and whoever does evil, it is against himself. Your Lord is no oppressor of His servants.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī renders the justice transparent: “for himself” — for the benefit returns to him; and whoever does evil wrongs only his own soul and harms only it, for it is he who suffers its damage and meets its evil. God “is no oppressor of His servants”: the penalty is never arbitrary, it is only the exact return of the act upon its author. The servant reaps what he has sown, and the divine balance never tips unjustly.

mots.racine: ع م ل / ص ل ح / ن ف س
mots.sens: who does good, it is for himself
mots.def: the benefit returns to the doer.

mots.racine: س و أ
mots.sens: who does evil, it is against him

mots.racine: ظ ل م / ع ب د
mots.sens: your Lord oppresses not the servants

versets.ar: إِلَيْهِ يُرَدُّ عِلْمُ السَّاعَةِ وَمَا تَخْرُجُ مِن ثَمَرَاتٍ مِّنْ أَكْمَامِهَا وَمَا تَحْمِلُ مِنْ أُنثَىٰ وَلَا تَضَعُ إِلَّا بِعِلْمِهِ وَيَوْمَ يُنَادِيهِمْ أَيْنَ شُرَكَائِي قَالُوا آذَنَّاكَ مَا مِنَّا مِن شَهِيدٍ
versets.tr: ilayhi yuraddu ʿilmu s-sāʿati wa-mā takhruju min thamarātin min akmāmihā wa-mā taḥmilu min unthā wa-lā taḍaʿu illā bi-ʿilmihī wa-yawma yunādīhim ayna shurakāʾī qālū ādhannāka mā minnā min shahīdin
versets.trad: To Him is referred the knowledge of the Hour. No fruit emerges from its sheath, no female conceives or gives birth, but with His knowledge. On the Day He calls to them: “Where are My partners?”, they will say: “We declare to You: there is no witness among us.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī answers those who pressed for the Hour: the knowledge of the Hour belongs to the Real alone; none other knows it. Just as none knows which fruits will come from the trees, nor what the wombs hold — boys or girls, and in what features —, so none knows when the Resurrection will arise. On the Day of the call “Where are My partners?”, the idolaters disavow their idols — but at an hour when the abundance of their remorse and their weeping avails them nothing.

mots.racine: ر د د / ع ل م / س و ع
mots.sens: to Him is referred the knowledge of the Hour

mots.racine: ث م ر / ك م م

mots.ar: وَمَا تَحْمِلُ مِنْ أُنثَىٰ وَلَا تَضَعُ
mots.tr: wa-mā taḥmilu min unthā wa-lā taḍaʿu
mots.racine: ح م ل / أ ن ث / و ض ع
mots.def: ḥamala: to carry; waḍaʿa: to give birth.

mots.racine: ش ه د

versets.ar: وَضَلَّ عَنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا يَدْعُونَ مِن قَبْلُ وَظَنُّوا مَا لَهُم مِّن مَّحِيصٍ
versets.tr: wa-ḍalla ʿanhum mā kānū yadʿūna min qablu wa-ẓannū mā lahum min maḥīṣin
versets.trad: And what they used to invoke before will forsake them, and they will be certain they have no escape.
versets.commentaire: What they invoked vanishes at the moment of need: Qushayrī reads in it the bankruptcy of every support apart from God. The idol, the false help, the thing set up in place of God — all of it strays away from the servant at the hour he thought to find refuge in it. And the belated certainty seizes them: no place to flee (maḥīṣ). Whoever did not make the Real his recourse in this world finds himself, there, without support or shelter.

mots.racine: ض ل ل
mots.def: ḍalla: to be lost, to vanish.

mots.racine: د ع و / ق ب ل
ح ي ص

versets.ar: لَّا يَسْأَمُ الْإِنسَانُ مِن دُعَاءِ الْخَيْرِ وَإِن مَّسَّهُ الشَّرُّ فَيَئُوسٌ قَنُوطٌ
versets.tr: lā yasʾamu l-insānu min duʿāʾi l-khayri wa-in massahu sh-sharru fa-yaʾūsun qanūṭun
versets.trad: Man does not weary of praying for good; but if evil touches him, he despairs and loses hope.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī depicts the imbalance of the soul left to itself: man does not weary of desiring good and safety; but if evil touches him, he despairs, no longer hoping for its end — through his lack of knowledge of his Lord and the closing of his heart's path back to Him. Despair is no fatality of trial but a symptom of ignorance: whoever knows his Lord never despairs of His mercy.

mots.racine: س أ م / أ ن س
د ع و / خ ي ر
م س س / ش ر ر
ي أ س / ق ن ط

versets.ar: وَلَئِنْ أَذَقْنَاهُ رَحْمَةً مِّنَّا مِن بَعْدِ ضَرَّاءَ مَسَّتْهُ لَيَقُولَنَّ هَٰذَا لِي وَمَا أَظُنُّ السَّاعَةَ قَائِمَةً وَلَئِن رُّجِعْتُ إِلَىٰ رَبِّي إِنَّ لِي عِندَهُ لَلْحُسْنَىٰ فَلَنُنَبِّئَنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِمَا عَمِلُوا وَلَنُذِيقَنَّهُم مِّنْ عَذَابٍ غَلِيظٍ
versets.tr: wa-la-in adhaqnāhu raḥmatan minnā min baʿdi ḍarrāʾa massathu la-yaqūlanna hādhā lī wa-mā aẓunnu s-sāʿata qāʾimatan wa-la-in rujiʿtu ilā rabbī inna lī ʿindahū la-l-ḥusnā fa-la-nunabbiʾanna lladhīna kafarū bi-mā ʿamilū wa-la-nudhīqannahum min ʿadhābin ghalīẓin
versets.trad: And if We make him taste a mercy from Us after an affliction that touched him, he surely says: “This is my due! I do not think the Hour will come; but if I am returned to my Lord, I shall surely have the best with Him.” We will inform the disbelievers of what they did, and We will make them taste a heavy punishment.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī lays bare the illusion of the ingrate: if We lift the trial from him and restore his hope, he attributes the merit to himself — by right or by chance — without believing it comes from Us as grace and favor. And he lends him this vain reasoning: “Were there a gathering and a return for me, I would have from God kindness and good” — but tomorrow he will know that the matter is far other than he imagined: at the instant We make him taste the punishment he has earned. To presume on the divine favor without faith is but pride in disguise.

mots.racine: ذ و ق / ر ح م
mots.sens: We make him taste a mercy

mots.racine: ق و ل
mots.sens: he surely says: this is my due

mots.racine: ظ ن ن / س و ع / ق و م
mots.sens: I do not think the Hour will come

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / غ ل ظ

versets.ar: وَإِذَا أَنْعَمْنَا عَلَى الْإِنسَانِ أَعْرَضَ وَنَأَىٰ بِجَانِبِهِ وَإِذَا مَسَّهُ الشَّرُّ فَذُو دُعَاءٍ عَرِيضٍ
versets.tr: wa-idhā anʿamnā ʿalā l-insāni aʿraḍa wa-naʾā bi-jānibihī wa-idhā massahu sh-sharru fa-dhū duʿāʾin ʿarīḍin
versets.trad: When We bestow favor upon man, he turns away and withdraws aside; but when evil touches him, he is full of prolonged supplication.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unmasks the man who cannot tell trial from gift: much of what he takes for favor is ruse and gradual lure (makr, istidrāj), and he lingers in it; much of what is grace and bounty he reckons a calamity, recoils from it and detests it. And he describes his to-and-fro: when favored, he grows proud, swells with arrogance, neither thanks his Lord nor recalls His bounty, and withdraws from the carpet of His obedience; but when evil touches him, behold him in profuse supplications, in endless imploring — then, the evil lifted, he returns to his rebellion and his ingratitude.

mots.racine: ن ع م / أ ن س
ع ر ض / ن أ ي / ج ن ب
mots.sens: he turns away and withdraws aside
mots.def: naʾā bi-jānibihī: to draw off in disdain.

mots.racine: د ع و / ع ر ض

versets.ar: قُلْ أَرَأَيْتُمْ إِن كَانَ مِنْ عِندِ اللَّهِ ثُمَّ كَفَرْتُم بِهِ مَنْ أَضَلُّ مِمَّنْ هُوَ فِي شِقَاقٍ بَعِيدٍ
versets.tr: qul a-raʾaytum in kāna min ʿindi llāhi thumma kafartum bihī man aḍallu mimman huwa fī shiqāqin baʿīdin
versets.trad: Say: “Have you considered: if it is from God and then you disbelieve in it — who is more astray than one in deep schism?”
versets.commentaire: “Who is more astray than one in deep schism?” Qushayrī lets one feel the absurdity of the unbeliever's wager: to refuse what may be from God is to expose oneself to the most utter loss for nothing. None is more astray than one who has cast himself into a far schism (shiqāq baʿīd), so far from the Truth that no call seems any longer to reach him. The schism of the heart hollows out a distance the proof can no longer cross.

mots.ar: أَرَأَيْتُمْ إِن كَانَ مِنْ عِندِ اللَّهِ
mots.tr: a-raʾaytum in kāna min ʿindi llāhi
mots.racine: ر أ ي / ك و ن
mots.sens: consider: if it is from God

mots.racine: ك ف ر
ض ل ل
ش ق ق / ب ع د

versets.ar: سَنُرِيهِمْ آيَاتِنَا فِي الْآفَاقِ وَفِي أَنفُسِهِمْ حَتَّىٰ يَتَبَيَّنَ لَهُمْ أَنَّهُ الْحَقُّ أَوَلَمْ يَكْفِ بِرَبِّكَ أَنَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ شَهِيدٌ
versets.tr: sa-nurīhim āyātinā fī l-āfāqi wa-fī anfusihim ḥattā yatabayyana lahum annahu l-ḥaqqu a-wa-lam yakfi bi-rabbika annahū ʿalā kulli shayʾin shahīdun
versets.trad: We will show them Our signs in the horizons and in their own souls, until it becomes clear to them that it is the Truth. Does it not suffice that your Lord is Witness over all things?
themes: the signs in the horizons and the souls
versets.commentaire: Here is one of the most meditated verses of the sūra. Qushayrī divides the two fields of unveiling: “in the horizons” for the scholars — the proofs of the world's origination and the demand of an Author for its attributes; “and in their own souls” for the people of gnosis — what they experience of punishment the moment they brush against a fault, and of reward the moment they purify an obedience. And he inscribes in it the very experience of the Sufi: the variations of states that befall them — contraction (qabḍ) and expansion (basṭ), union (jamʿ) and separation (farq), veiling and attraction — all that they find of necessity in their dealings and their halts: these are the signs in the souls. As for “your Lord suffices as Witness,” he slips in a final allusion: He is the Sufficient; but the common folk remain in doubt of what the Real unveils to the people of presence among the teachings of the secret.

versets.ar: أَلَا إِنَّهُمْ فِي مِرْيَةٍ مِّن لِّقَاءِ رَبِّهِمْ أَلَا إِنَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ مُّحِيطٌ
versets.tr: alā innahum fī miryatin min liqāʾi rabbihim alā innahū bi-kulli shayʾin muḥīṭun
versets.trad: Behold, they are in doubt about the meeting with their Lord. Behold, He encompasses all things.
versets.commentaire: The sūra seals upon the deniers' doubt concerning “the meeting with their Lord.” Qushayrī joins a last allusion on the degrees: they are in uncertainty about the meeting on the Day of Resurrection; and the common folk remain in doubt that the people of presence may even be permitted to receive, here below, the unveilings of the secret. But the verse closes on encompassment: “He encompasses all things” (muḥīṭ) — He knows all, nothing escapes Him. Man's doubt in no way diminishes the knowledge that envelops him on every side.
